VAL-API-003.reason: issues(first: 2) returned exactly 2 nodes in { nodes: [...] } connection shape. With 18+ issues in DB, response contained only INV-1 and INV-2.

VAL-API-004.reason: issues(filter: { team: { key: { eq: "INV" } } }) returned all 18 INV-prefixed issues. Same filter with "APP" returned { nodes: [] } since APP team has no issues. All returned issues have INV- identifiers confirming team isolation.

VAL-API-005.reason: issues(filter: { state: { name: { eq: "In Progress" } } }) returned exactly 3 issues (INV-1, INV-3, INV-11), all with state.name: "In Progress". No issues in other states were included.

VAL-API-006.reason: issues(filter: { assignee: { isMe: { eq: true } } }) returned 4 issues (INV-1, INV-3, INV-4, INV-11), all with assignee.isMe: true, assignee.name: "Admin". No unassigned issues were included.

VAL-API-007.reason: issues(filter: { labels: { some: { name: { in: ["task", "epic"] } } } }) returned 5 issues. Each has at least one of "task" or "epic" labels: INV-3 (task), INV-4 (task), INV-5 (Feature+task), INV-8 (epic+task), INV-11 (epic). Issues with only "blocked" or no labels were excluded.

VAL-API-008.reason: issues(filter: { labels: { every: { name: { nin: ["blocked", "needs-clarification"] } } } }) returned 16 issues. INV-12 (blocked label) and INV-16 (blocked label) were correctly excluded. No returned issue has "blocked" or "needs-clarification" labels.

VAL-API-009.reason: Compound and filter combining team.key.eq(INV) + state.name.eq(In Progress) + assignee.isMe.eq(true) + labels.some.name.in([task,epic]) + labels.every.name.nin([blocked,needs-clarification]) returned exactly 2 issues: INV-3 (task label, In Progress, assigned to Admin) and INV-11 (epic label, In Progress, assigned to Admin). All 5 conditions are satisfied conjunctively.

VAL-API-010.reason: issues(filter: { state: { name: { eq: "NonExistentState" } } }) returned { nodes: [] } with no errors. HTTP 200, valid JSON response.

VAL-API-011.reason: children { nodes { id } } works on both single issue queries and in issues list. INV-14 (parent) has children.nodes with 1 child (INV-15). INV-13 (no children) has children.nodes: []. Issues in list query also expose children.nodes correctly.

VAL-API-036.reason: issues(filter: { labels: { some: { name: { in: [] } } } }) returned { nodes: [] } without error. Empty in array handled gracefully - no issue can match an empty inclusion list.

VAL-API-037.reason: issues(filter: { labels: { every: { name: { nin: [] } } } }) returned all 18 issues. Empty nin array acts as a no-op filter, excluding nothing. Count matches unfiltered query.

VAL-API-038.reason: Used GraphQL aliases to compare direct filter vs and-wrapped filter in one request. Both 'direct' and 'wrapped' returned identical 18-issue node arrays with same IDs in same order. and: [{ team: { key: { eq: "INV" } } }] behaves identically to direct filter.

VAL-API-039.reason: issue(id: INV-13) returned assignee: null. The unassigned issue correctly resolves assignee as null, not an empty object or error.

VAL-API-040.reason: issue(id: INV-13) returned labels: { nodes: [] }. Issue with no labels correctly returns empty nodes array, not null or error.

frictions.description: The `issues` query requires a `first` argument (Int!) - it is not optional. Discovered when trying issues without first parameter, which returned a validation error.
frictions.resolution: Always pass first: N when querying issues.

frictions.description: APP team has no workflow states, so issue creation in APP team fails with success: false. This limits cross-team filter testing.
frictions.resolution: Verified team filter by confirming INV filter returns all INV issues and APP filter returns empty nodes. Filter correctness confirmed.

summary: Tested 14 assertions: 14 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All issue filter assertions (VAL-API-003 through VAL-API-011, VAL-API-036 through VAL-API-040) pass. The filter engine correctly handles first-limit, team/state/assignee/label filters, compound and-filters, empty arrays, children connection shape, null assignee, and empty label nodes.
